description:
Files: runtime/src/heap.rs

Replace the single block with two:
  young_start: *mut u8
  young_top: *mut u8
  young_end: *mut u8
  young_size_class: u8       // small pool block, target ~64KB
  
  old_start: *mut u8
  old_top: *mut u8
  old_end: *mut u8
  old_size_class: u8         // larger pool block, initial ~512KB
  
  minor_gc_count: u8         // triggers major after 10+ minors
  last_minor_live_bytes: usize
  last_major_live_bytes: usize

Heap::new(): allocate one young block (pick SIZE_TABLE entry nearest 64KB) and one old block (nearest 512KB). Retire block_start/bump_top/block_end/size_class/low_live_streak fields (last_gc_live_bytes splits into last_minor_live_bytes / last_major_live_bytes).

Heap::alloc(): bump young_top. gc_watermark points into young block at 75% of young_size. FZ_SHOULD_YIELD check unchanged.

Heap::bytes_used(): young_top - young_start + old_top - old_start.

Tests: alloc() fills young block; watermark fires at 75% of young block; old block is unaffected by young allocs.
title: gen-gc.1: Heap struct — young + old blocks, alloc into young, young watermark
